title: Add EntityObservedEvent and EntityDepartedEvent to WorldEvents.cs
description:
Create typed world event records for entity observations so the C# pipeline can process them.

Implementation:
1. Add `EntityObservedEvent` record to `Agent.Core/Events/WorldEvents.cs`:
   - `string EntityType` — e.g. "cow", "zombie", "skeleton", "player"
   - `string? DisplayName` — custom name if named (e.g. "Dinnerbone")
   - `string? MobCategory` — "hostile", "passive", "neutral", "player"
   - `Position Position` — floored block position of the entity
   - `double Distance` — Euclidean distance from bot
   - `double? Health` — current HP if available
   - `DateTimeOffset Timestamp`
2. Add `EntityDepartedEvent` record:
   - `string EntityType` — the type that left
   - `Position LastPosition` — last known position
   - `DateTimeOffset Timestamp`
3. Add `EntityDamagedEvent` or extend existing `DamageTakenEvent` if the bot is damaged by a nearby entity (future: track which entity caused damage). For now, this can be deferred.

Acceptance:
- Records compile and are usable in pattern matching.
- `EntityObservedEvent` carries enough data to summarize for the LLM.
- No breaking changes to existing event types.

Refs: `memorysmith_situational_awareness_design_doc_20260625T020914Z.md` Phase 1
